brains.name: Charlie Munger
brains.source: Poor Charlie's Almanack (his collected speeches and talks), 40+ years of Berkshire Hathaway shareholder Q&A, Wesco and Daily Journal annual meetings, the 1995 'Psychology of Human Misjudgment' speech at Harvard, the 1986 Harvard School commencement address, the 2007 USC Law commencement address, and extensive press interviews from 1962 through 2023

brains.name: Hank Green
brains.source: 600+ SciShow episodes, 18+ years of Vlogbrothers, Crash Course series, Dear Hank & John podcast, TikTok science videos (7M+ followers), TED talks, keynotes, interviews, Wikipedia profile, NPR interviews, TIME Magazine feature, New York Times coverage, and two novels (An Absolutely Remarkable Thing, A Beautifully Foolish Endeavor)

brains.name: John Green
brains.source: 36 episodes of The Anthropocene Reviewed podcast, 44 book essays, 7 novels, 2 nonfiction books, 2 TED talks, multiple commencement addresses, BookCon panels (2015, 2019), NPR Fresh Air and Wild Card interviews, Freakonomics interview, New Yorker profile (2014), and 18+ years of Vlogbrothers videos (2007–present)

brains.name: Paul Graham
brains.source: 220+ essays spanning 2001-2025, plus interviews with Tyler Cowen, Geoff Ralston, The Pull Request, YC lectures, and talks at Startup School and Disrupt

brains.name: Peter Attia
brains.source: 400+ episodes of The Drive podcast, reviews and interviews about Outlive (2023), TED talk on metabolic health, Joe Rogan Experience episodes #1108 and #1961, Huberman Lab appearances, and detailed blog posts

brains.name: Sun Tzu
brains.source: 13 chapters of The Art of War, Wikipedia profile, Sonshi analysis, multiple documentary and audiobook video sources, and centuries of military and strategic commentary attributed to Sun Tzu and his school of thought

brains.name: Jensen Huang

brains.name: Dario Amodei

brains.source: Public talks, interviews, podcast appearances, and writings across Shiva Rajaraman's career from YouTube through OpenAI
